title: Ligation with T4 DNA ligase (NEB)
description: T4 DNA ligase reaction setup and incubation (sticky vs blunt) per the NEB M0202 protocol.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Join a vector and insert (or any compatible DNA ends) with T4 DNA ligase, then transform. The example reaction uses a 1:3 vector:insert molar ratio.

## Materials

| Item | Amount (20 uL reaction) |
| --- | --- |
| T4 DNA Ligase Buffer, 10X (thaw at room temperature; contains ATP) | 2 uL |
| Vector DNA (4 kb) | 50 ng (0.020 pmol) |
| Insert DNA (1 kb) | 37.5 ng (0.060 pmol) |
| Nuclease-free water | to 20 uL |
| T4 DNA Ligase | 1 uL |

## Steps

1. Set up the reaction on ice in the order above. Add the T4 DNA Ligase last. Use NEBioCalculator to set the molar ratio (the table shows 1:3 vector:insert).
2. Mix gently by pipetting up and down and spin down briefly.
3. Cohesive (sticky) ends: incubate at 16 C overnight, or at room temperature for 10 min.
4. Blunt ends or single-base overhangs: incubate at 16 C overnight, or at room temperature for 2 h.
5. Heat-inactivate at 65 C for 10 min.
6. Chill on ice and transform 1-5 uL into 50 uL competent cells.

## Expected result

Ligated product that yields colonies after transformation. Run a vector-only (no insert) control to gauge background self-ligation.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- A 1:3 vector:insert molar ratio is typical; 1:1 to 1:10 works for single insertions. For blunt ends, NEB recommends an insert:vector ratio of 2-6.
- Thaw the 10X buffer at room temperature; its ATP is labile and degrades with repeated freeze-thaw.
- High background: dephosphorylate the vector or add a no-insert control to quantify it.

## Safety

BSL-1. Standard molecular-biology reagents.

## Source

Adapted from the NEB T4 DNA Ligase ligation protocol (M0202).
https://www.neb.com/en-us/protocols/dna-ligation-with-t4-dna-ligase-m0202
